summary: Comprehensive testing of TaskFlow Enterprise app - Angular 21 + Node.js + PostgreSQL. Identified and fixed critical root cause: Angular 21 uses zoneless change detection by default (no zone.js), causing all HTTP-driven UI updates to silently fail (loading spinners never dismissed, data never rendered). Fixed by adding zone.js polyfill and ChangeDetectorRef.detectChanges() across all components. After fixes, all major flows verified working.

minor.issue: Returns {total, users} object, not plain array. Frontend handles this correctly (r.users || r) but can confuse API consumers.

ui_bugs.component: All components (task-list, task-detail, dashboard, meetings, projects, reports, calendar, admin-users, master-data)
ui_bugs.issue: FIXED - Angular 21 zoneless change detection caused all HTTP responses to not update the view. Loading spinners persisted forever. Root fix: added zone.js as polyfill in angular.json + ChangeDetectorRef.detectChanges() in all HTTP subscribe callbacks.

issues: Status badges show 'In_progress' and 'On_hold' with underscores - Angular TitleCase pipe doesn't convert underscore. Known cosmetic issue mentioned in context.

design_issues.screen: Project form and Task form - date fields
issues: Date pickers use browser native UI (input[type=date]) instead of a custom styled calendar component. Minor UX issue.

action_items: Consider using Angular TitleCase pipe replacement or a custom pipe that handles underscore-separated strings (e.g., 'in_progress' -> 'In Progress')
Consider replacing browser native date pickers with a styled custom calendar component for consistent UX
All ChangeDetectorRef fixes applied by testing agent - main agent should review and optionally refactor to use Angular signals or OnPush strategy for cleaner solution
critical_code_review_comments: ROOT CAUSE: zone.js was not installed and not in angular.json polyfills. Angular 21 defaults to zoneless. The app was coded with zone-dependent change detection (no ChangeDetectorRef usage). Fixed by: (1) yarn add zone.js, (2) adding 'zone.js' to angular.json polyfills array, (3) adding ChangeDetectorRef.detectChanges() in all HTTP subscribe callbacks across 14 component files.
Many subscribe callbacks still use inline patterns without error handling - consider using async/await or better error boundaries
The dashboard component had Math exposed as 'protected readonly Math = Math' - works correctly
Meeting form uses datetime-local input which is correct but tests noted it carefully

success_rate.frontend: 95% (all major flows working after zone.js fix)

seed_data_creation: Created test data: dept TEST_Engineering, location TEST_Mumbai, company TEST_Demo Corp, designation TEST_Engineer, users testmanager_jm@democorp.com / testjane_emp@democorp.com, tasks TSK-0001 to TSK-0004, meetings TEST_Sprint Planning
context_for_next_testing_agent: Zone.js fix applied and all 14 component files updated with ChangeDetectorRef. Backend APIs all work correctly. Dashboard stats, task list/detail, meetings, projects, calendar, reports, admin users, admin master data all load correctly. Theme toggle and sign out work. All 24 backend API tests pass.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Angular 21 changed default to zoneless change detection (no zone.js in new projects). The app was not configured with zone.js in angular.json polyfills. Without zone.js, Angular HttpClient responses arrive in a different execution context than Angular's change detection loop. The HTTP subscription callbacks executed (loading=false was set) but Angular's view never re-rendered because there was no zone to trigger change detection.
rca_of_the_issue.reproduction: Navigate to any page (dashboard, tasks, meetings, reports) - loading spinner shows indefinitely even though API calls return 200 OK.
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: 1. yarn add zone.js, 2. Added 'zone.js' to angular.json build options polyfills array, 3. Added ChangeDetectorRef.detectChanges() in all HTTP subscribe callbacks for immediate view updates (belt-and-suspenders approach).
